final_text:
The bug is clear. In `to_python`, the `ValidationError` is raised without passing a `params` dict containing the value, so `%(value)s` is never substituted. Compare with how `ModelMultipleChoiceField` does it (with `params={'value': value}`).

{"file":"django/forms/models.py","class":"ModelChoiceField","method":"to_python","parent_class":"ChoiceField","fix_summary":"The ValidationError raised in to_python must pass a params dict containing the invalid value (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the error message is substituted with the actual submitted value."}

parsed_answer.fix_summary: The ValidationError raised in to_python must pass a params dict containing the invalid value (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the error message is substituted with the actual submitted value.
